iterations.hypothesis: USDC (listing 1) and USDE (listing 28) spread is mean-reverting; tick-based EWMA z-score with alpha=0.99, entry_z=1.0, close_z=0.2, max_hold=60s.
changes: Initial strategy: StablecoinStatArb based on NExchangeArb, entry_z=1.0, close_z=0.2, ewma_alpha=0.99, max_hold=60s
iterations.analysis: 90k+ fills in 3 days. Tick-based EWMA alpha=0.99 has half-life of only 22s at 3.16 ticks/sec. Fires constantly on noise. Systematic losses.
iterations.next_action: Increase entry_z to 2.5, set close_z=0.0 to exit only when spread crosses mean.

iterations.hypothesis: Higher entry_z=2.5 filters noise; close_z=0.0 ensures exit only on true mean crossing; max_hold 10min.
iterations.analysis: 79% improvement but still losing. Tick-based EWMA std too small (~0.22 bps) so z=2.5 fires at only 0.55 bps above mean. EWMA adapts in 22s, close fires on mean drift not spread reversion.
iterations.next_action: Replace tick-based EWMA with time-weighted EWMA (halflife=300s). Lock entry reference.

iterations.hypothesis: Time-weighted EWMA (halflife=300s) gives a meaningful z-score. Locked entry reference prevents false closes.
iterations.analysis: Breakthrough: PnL +4.175, pct_positive_buckets=1.0. 1716 round trips at +0.00243 each. Latency insensitive.
iterations.next_action: Sweep halflife, entry_z, close_z to optimize.

iterations.hypothesis: Lower close_z captures more reversion; shorter halflife and lower entry_z increase trade frequency.
iterations.analysis: 340% PnL improvement (4.175->18.38). Monotonic trends: close_z=-0.5 beats 0.0 everywhere; shorter halflife better; lower entry_z better. Optima suggest lying outside tested grid.

iterations.hypothesis: Continuing trend: even shorter halflife and lower entry_z; close_z=-1.0 may hold trades to deeper reversion for more edge per round trip.
iterations.analysis: 36% PnL improvement (18.38->25.01). close_z=-1.0 dominates close_z=-0.5 in EVERY parameter combination by ~5 PnL units — holding to deeper reversion consistently captures more spread. PnL/round-trip improved 35% (0.00344->0.00466) with same trade frequency. Halflife trend less clear: optimal at 90s with 60s and 120s close behind; halflife=30s is not better despite earlier trend — suggests diminishing returns below ~60s. entry_z=1.0 is optimal: 0.75 generates too many marginal trades (more fills, lower PnL/RT), 1.25+ too selective. close_z trend still monotonically decreasing — optima may lie further below -1.0. User hint: sweep size to find slippage breakeven.
iterations.next_action: Per user hint: sweep size=[1000,2000,5000,10000,20000,50000] at best params (halflife=90, entry_z=1.0, close_z=-1.0) to find where slippage starts degrading PnL/round-trip.

iterations.hypothesis: Per user request: sweep size=[1000,2000,5000,10000,20000,50000] at best params (halflife=90, entry_z=1.0, close_z=-1.0) to find where slippage starts degrading PnL/round-trip.
changes: Remote sweep: size=[1000,2000,5000,10000,20000,50000], all other params fixed at iter 5 best
iterations.analysis: Strategy is capacity-constrained, not slippage-constrained. Fill count collapses with size: 21430 at size=1000 → 72 at size=10000 (99.7% drop). The risk_averse queue model places us last in queue; larger orders rarely fill at the taker prices we target. Per-RT PnL artificially rises with size (only the most extreme spreads trigger fills) but total PnL degrades sharply. size=1000 maximizes total PnL (25.01). size=50000 goes negative (6 fills, adverse selection on the rare fills that do happen). Confirmed: size=1000 is the capacity ceiling for this strategy.
iterations.next_action: Size confirmed at 1000. Continue optimizing close_z (trend still monotonic: 0.0 > -0.5 > -1.0) — try close_z=[-1.5,-2.0,-2.5] at best params. Also test max_position=[5,10,20] to allow more concurrent positions and increase frequency.

iterations.hypothesis: Deeper close_z may capture more reversion per trade; higher max_position may allow more concurrent positions for higher frequency.
changes: Remote sweep: close_z=[-1.0,-1.5,-2.0,-2.5], max_position=[5,10,20], all other params fixed at best
iterations.analysis: Two decisive findings: (1) max_position is completely irrelevant — identical results at 5/10/20. The strategy only opens one position at a time (both legs must be flat before entry), so position stacking never occurs. (2) close_z=-1.0 is the total-PnL optimum. Deeper thresholds reduce fill count faster than they improve per-trade edge: close_z=-1.5 halves fills but only reduces PnL by 19%. Hold time analysis confirms max_hold_ns is not binding: 97.4% of trades close in <300s, only 1.1% hit the 10-min cap. Spread reverts fast (median 17.9s), consistent with halflife=90s EWMA. No further gains from close_z or max_position dimensions.
iterations.next_action: Parameters now converged on: halflife=90, entry_z=1.0, close_z=-1.0, size=1000. Fine-tune entry side: sweep entry_z=[0.75,0.875,1.0,1.125,1.25] x min_spread_bps=[0.25,0.5,0.75] to see if lower spread filter or slightly different entry threshold can squeeze more trades or better edge.

iterations.hypothesis: Lower min_spread_bps filter allows more entry signals; finer entry_z grid may reveal better threshold than 1.0.
changes: Remote sweep: entry_z=[0.75,0.875,1.0,1.125,1.25], min_spread_bps=[0.25,0.5,0.75], all other params fixed at best
iterations.analysis: Marginal gains only (+3.7% PnL). min_spread_bps=0.25 beats 0.5 and 0.75 consistently — lower filter admits slightly more trades. entry_z=1.0 remains optimal even at finer resolution. Total PnL range across all 15 jobs is only 22.99–25.93 (<13%), confirming parameter convergence. Three consecutive iterations (6,7,8) show <5% PnL improvement — plateau reached on parameter space. Next step requires a structural code change: add an entry momentum/confirmation filter to reduce false entries where spread is still widening at entry.
iterations.next_action: Structural change: add entry confirmation filter. When z-score crosses entry_z, only enter if the z-score has peaked (current z < max z over last N ticks), confirming spread has started reverting. This should reduce adverse entries where spread continues widening post-entry. Try N=[3,5,10] ticks as sweep parameter.

hypothesis: Entry confirmation filter: only enter when z-score has peaked (current abs_z < max abs_z over last N ticks), ensuring spread has started reverting before entry. Should improve PnL/RT by avoiding entries at the top of widening moves.
changes: Remote sweep: reversion_ticks=[0,3,5,10], all other params at iter 8 best (min_spread_bps=0.25)
analysis: Entry reversion filter works: +6.6% PnL (25.93->27.64). Confirming that z-score has peaked before entry improves PnL/RT 9.2% (0.00472->0.00516) with minimal fill count reduction (21970->21450). The filter eliminates entries where the spread is still widening, ensuring we enter closer to the true peak. reversion_ticks=3 is optimal: 5 and 10 are nearly identical (27.44/27.43) suggesting the peak confirmation only needs 3 ticks of evidence. First >5% improvement since iter 5, breaking the plateau.
next_action: Fine-tune: sweep reversion_ticks=[1,2,3,4,5] x entry_z=[0.875,1.0,1.125] to find if reversion_ticks=1 or 2 might be better, and whether entry_z changes optimally with the filter. 15 jobs.
